title: The old Syracuse Developmental Center will soon be a residential space, if approved
summary: The City of Syracuse's seized Syracuse Developmental Center property is undergoing demolition and redevelopment by the Albanese Organization into a mixed-income residential community. Phase one includes two multi-family buildings and 27 townhomes with affordable to market-rate pricing, pending Common Council approval of financing and state funding. Construction is targeted to begin in early 2026.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Demonstrates city commitment to addressing housing supply and neighborhood redevelopment through public-private partnership; requires financing coordination and state funding.

processing_notes: Author: Hannah Devanny; Published by CNY Central (NBC/CBS affiliate); No specific dollar amounts provided; Financial approval pending with Common Council; State funding component mentioned but not quantified; Timeline: demolition completion October 2025, construction start early 2026
